content:
Five experts who built leading AI prototyping tools share a surprising common practice despite different backgrounds and workflows. Though they start from unique points, all emphasize deliberate constraints to guide AI effectively.

• Sachin Rekhi uses real product screenshots to build reusable, styled templates, ensuring prototypes reflect actual design systems instead of generic frameworks.  
• Boris Cherny generates dozens of variations and applies an 80% kill rate, treating elimination as a core part of creative refinement.  
• Colin Matthews commands Bolt to "make a plan, don’t write code," significantly improving output quality through structured planning.  
• Dan Olsen begins with a mini-PRD and Nadav Abrahami stresses clarity, both ensuring the AI avoids drift and misinterpretation through early constraints.
